title: SKOS Relation Triple Builder
description: Given six unlabeled concept-pair prompts drawn from this book's own vocabulary, the learner can correctly select and orient the SKOS relation type (broader, narrower, or related) for each pair and receive immediate correct/incorrect feedback.
creator: Learning Graphs
author: Dan McCreary
subject: Learning Graphs

learningObjectives: Select the correct SKOS relation type for an unseen concept pair
Orient a broader/narrower relation in the correct direction
Explain why broader and narrower are inverses while related is symmetric
Distinguish a hierarchical relation from an associative one

pedagogical.teachingStrategy: Build-and-check practice with immediate feedback and first-try scoring
keyQuestions: If two learners answer the same pair with different relations but opposite arrow directions, can both be right?
What does swapping the arrow do to a broader relation? To a related relation?
Is skos:broader a property of a pair of concepts, or of an ordered pair?
Why would a validator accept a backward triple even though it asserts the opposite of what you meant?
commonMisconceptions: That picking the right relation type is the whole answer — a correct type on a backward arrow is still wrong
That broader and narrower are different relations rather than the same hierarchy read from opposite ends
That any two concepts in the same subject area can be linked with broader/narrower — sibling concepts like two graph metrics need related instead
assessmentOpportunities: Record each learner's first-try score across the six pairs
Have half the class answer pair 2 as drawn and half swap first, then compare answers
Ask learners to write a seventh pair with its correct relation and justify the direction
